perturbed_task_language: Pick the alphabet soup and place it in the basket
strategy_notes: Frame: OBJECT (eef home z=0.261, table z~0.003). Identified alphabet_soup visually in image_cam_00.png as the blue/yellow Campbell's-style can in the back-left of the scene (vs the green/red tomato_sauce in the front-left). Coarse agentview localization: world_00.npy sampled at pixel (115,68), (118,72), (120,65) → median can-top world xy ~(-0.116, -0.222), top z ~0.072. Pre-positioned eef at (-0.10, -0.22, 0.18); wrist refine via world_wrist_10.npy mask z in [0.05,0.10] over lower-center crop → median (-0.116, -0.238, 0.072) — authoritative grasp xy. Basket: sampled (110,210)/(115,215)/(115,200) → center ~(-0.05, +0.30), rim z ~0.11. First attempt with sub-instruction prompt 'pick up the alphabet soup' + lift_thresh=0.05 reported success=true but actually closed empty (gripper qpos=0.007 ≈ shut) — false positive heuristic; reset. Second attempt with full task language and max_chunks=10 lifted the can (qpos gap 3cm = holding), but capped Pi0 before it could end-to-end the place (kept Rule 1). LLM scripted move_to via mid-waypoint (-0.05, 0.20, 0.30) then over-basket (-0.05, 0.29, 0.28), release fired libero_terminated=true.
